titles.title: No Tax on Large Party Tips Act
No Tax on Large Party Tips Act
A bill to clarify the treatment of certain tips for purposes of the tax deduction for qualified tips.

action.text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.

bill.summary:
No Tax on Large Party Tips Act
This bill expands the federal tax deduction for qualified tip income to include tips that are automatically added to a customer's bill and tips that are suggested tips prompted by a business by treating such tips as voluntarily paid.
As background, for 2025 through 2028, individuals may claim a tax deduction of up to $25,000 per year for qualified tips received in occupations that customarily and regularly receive tips. However, one of the conditions for claiming the tax credit is that the tips must be voluntarily, determined by the customer, and nonnegotiable.
